title: Karavis

{{Quote|With Operation Ringbreaker meeting early success, I feel our current angle of attack could provide a broader path to victory. This map notes the locations of other major Imperial shipyards and drydocks, as well as key mining centers for metals and ores. Once we take out Kuat, these should be our next targets.|Crix Madine, in an official document to Mon Mothma|<ref name="Rebel Files" />}}
The [[Outer Rim Territories]] celestial body '''Karavis''' was a major industrial site for the [[Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War]]. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] [[General]] [[Crix Madine]] identified it as a possible target in the event that the Alliance campaign [[Operation Ringbreaker]] was successful.

==Description==
Karavis was a celestial body<ref name="Rebel Files" /> located in the [[Obtrexta sector]]'s [[Karavis system]] within an intersecting portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="Systems" /> and the [[New Territories]].<ref name="New Territories" /> It lay in the grid square K-4 on the [[Standard Galactic Grid]].<ref name="Systems" />

==History==
[[File:MadineHS-ROTJ.png|thumb|left|200px|Crix Madine marked Karavis as a possible target for the Rebel Alliance.]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]], Karavis was a major industrial site for the Empire.<ref name="Rebel Files" /> In [[3 ABY]],<ref name="Timelines">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''</ref> the Alliance's [[61st Mobile Infantry]] attacked a number of minor Imperial resource hubs to weaken the defenses of the major [[Shipyard|shipbuilding]] hub [[Kuat Drive Yards]] for a final attack, a campaign dubbed [[Operation Ringbreaker]].<ref name="BTC">''[[Battlefront: Twilight Company]]''</ref> While [[Rebel Alliance Intelligence Service|Alliance Intelligence]] vetted the campaign from afar, Alliance [[General]] [[Crix Madine]] observed Operation Ringbreaker's early success and drew up a [[The galaxy|galactic]] [[map]] of other major Imperial shipyards, drydocks, and [[mining]] centers that the Alliance could target if Operation Ringbreaker was successful.<ref name="Rebel Files" />

Included on the map, Karavis was marked as a low-security, medium-priority option. The map was added to an official document on Madine's ideas that was sent to [[Mon Mothma]], the Alliance's [[Commander-in-Chief]]. Soon afterward, the [[archivist]] [[Hendri Underholt]] included the map in a compilation of non-electronic documents called ''[[The Rebel Files]]''.<ref name="Rebel Files" /> The 61st ultimately abandoned Operation Ringbreaker before they could attack Kuat Drive Yards.<ref name="BTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Karavis was introduced in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] via a mention in the [[2017]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Rebel Files" /> In the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, the [[Karavis system]] was first mentioned in "[[The Galaxy Dragon and crew|The ''Galaxy Dragon'' and crew]]," an article written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] as a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Dragon Magazine 200|200th issue]] of the [[Dragon (magazine)|''Dragon'']] magazine in [[December]] [[1993]]. Since the article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Galaxy Dragon">{{DragonCite|200|The Galaxy Dragon and crew|The ''Galaxy Dragon'' and crew}}</ref> it is considered non-canon with respect to the Legends continuity.<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[Star system/Legends|star system]] was ultimately canonized within Legends via the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', authored by Wallace and [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Sources==
